description: Fix two bugs in minimax-agent: (1) getWebpackRequire() calls chunk.push() on every API invocation, causing unbounded webpack chunk array growth; (2) hardcoded webpack module ID 33993 breaks all tools whenever MiniMax deploys an app update.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/minimax-agent && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Cache the webpack require function to avoid unbounded chunk.push() calls
userStories.description: In plugins/minimax-agent/src/minimax-api.ts, getWebpackRequire() calls chunk.push() on every invocation to probe for the webpack require function. Since getAxios() calls getWebpackRequire() on every apiGet/apiPost call, every tool invocation appends a new entry to the global webpackChunk_N_E array, causing unbounded memory growth. Cache the require function in a module-level variable so chunk.push() is only called once.
acceptanceCriteria: chunk.push() is called at most once per adapter lifetime (not once per tool call)
Subsequent calls to getWebpackRequire() return the cached require function without probing webpack again
The function still returns null if the webpack chunk is unavailable on first call

userStories.title: Replace hardcoded webpack module ID 33993 with dynamic module discovery
userStories.description: In plugins/minimax-agent/src/minimax-api.ts, the Axios instance is accessed via the hardcoded webpack module ID req(33993). This ID is assigned at MiniMax's build time and changes whenever MiniMax deploys an app update, causing all tools to throw 'MiniMax Agent app not ready' errors. Replace the hardcoded ID with a scan of webpack modules to find the Axios instance by checking for the characteristic .get/.post interface.
acceptanceCriteria: The hardcoded numeric literal 33993 no longer appears in minimax-api.ts
The Axios instance is found by scanning webpack module exports for a recognized Axios interface (presence of .get and .post functions)
getAxios() still returns null if no matching module is found
